title: the most POWERFUL claude code feature in months
summary: Graphify is a non-RAG solution that creates knowledge graphs from code bases to enable more accurate and token-efficient responses. It addresses memory and context limitations when working with large codebases in Claude Code.
writeup: This video breaks down Claude Code's Ultra Code mode and dynamic workflows, showing how they differ from the default single-context-window approach by spawning multiple sub-agents, each with a fresh context window and a focused goal. The central argument is that long, complex tasks degrade in a single session due to context rot: agentic laziness, self-preferential bias, and goal drift. Ultra Code addresses this by automatically deciding when a dynamic workflow is warranted and building a custom harness tuned to the specific task. A live deep research demo using the built-in /deep-research workflow illustrates how the fan-out-and-synthesize pattern gathers, cross-references, and verifies information before producing a final report.

key_points: Ultra Code is activated via /effort inside Claude Code, setting effort to 'extra high' and enabling automatic dynamic workflow orchestration so that complex prompts trigger a custom multi-agent harness without the user explicitly invoking /workflow each time.

The core problem dynamic workflows solve is context rot in long single-session tasks: agentic laziness (Claude only partially completes large scopes), self-preferential bias (Claude cannot reliably judge its own output within the same session), and goal drifting on complex multi-step work.

A dynamic workflow builds a task-specific harness at runtime rather than applying a static default approach; for a checkout-migration question, for example, this means separate agents reading billing code, checking provider documentation, pricing transaction volume, and running a devil's-advocate review before producing a targeted recommendation.

Anthropic's blog 'A Harness for Every Task: Dynamic Workflows in Claude Code' documents the main workflow patterns: classify and act (routing subtasks to appropriate agents), fan out and synthesize (parallel sourcing then cross-referenced verification), adversarial verification, loop-until-done, tournament-style selection, and generate-and-filter.
Claude Code ships a built-in /deep-research workflow that implements the fan-out-and-synthesize pattern out of the box, and completed workflows can be saved and reused as replayable skills.
